items.title: U.S. approves nearly $2 billion in weapons sales to Saudi Arabia
items.description: This proposed sale will support the foreign policy and national security objectives of the United States by improving the security of a Major non-NATO Ally that is a force for political stability and economic progress in the Gulf Region, says U.S. State Department

items.title: Rubik’s Cube stars shine at European speedcubing event in the Netherlands
items.description: More than 1,000 speedcubers from 53 countries are competing in Arnhem, the Netherlands, from 16 to 19 July 2026. Events range from blindfolded solves to solving while wearing oven gloves.

How motherhood has changed things for BMX racer Lauren Reynolds
items.description: Lauren Reynolds is embracing her tag as one of the most experienced racers at this year's UCI BMX World Championships in Brisbane, her first as a mother.

items.title: Japanese sake brewery to make foray into Indian market
items.description: Takahashi Shouten, based in the city of Yame in Fukuoka Prefecture, launched a subsidiary in India in February.

items.title: Canberra childcare centre closes suddenly, worker sacked
items.description: Parents at Edge Early Learning in Higgins received an email last night advising the regulator had ordered the centre to immediately shut.

items.title: U.S. Mint will produce $1 coin with Trump's image, Treasury Secretary says
items.description: The design, which also features the words “Liberty,” “In God We Trust” and “1776-2026” on the ‌front and an image of the bald eagle from the presidential seal on ‌the back, was a revision from a ‌draft ⁠made public in October.

items.description: Neill died in Sydney on Monday, with his family saying his death was 'sudden and unexpected'.

items.title: Whiskey, wine makers sound alarm for ancient trade of coopering

items.title: Kenyan Rastafarians disappointed after court ruling denies cannabis use

items.title: No support for Jews fleeing Nazi Germany at Evian conference
items.description: In July 1938, a conference was held in Evian, France, to address the issue of Jews being persecuted by the Nazis in Germany and Austria. Almost nobody in the world was willing to take them in as refugees.

items.description: The delay would prevent the rate hike from being interpreted "as an attempt to influence the outcome" of the elections, Lula’s right-wing rival said.
